behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/range resolver/function-evaluator handoff, not just SUM arithmetic. MOCK-MISS: a pure SUM unit test can pass while range binding is wrong. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP do not apply. Rejected System because behavior text is only in-engine evaluation with no OS/network/subprocess requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real workbook engine in-memory, set A1..A10 values, set target cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run recalculation, and assert the computed value. Use real parser, reference resolver, and function registry together (no mocked resolver/evaluator boundaries).

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on cross-cell formula chaining (module boundary: dependency graph + evaluator + reference resolution). Unit on one expression would miss cascade/handoff defects. No real OS/network/subprocess and no AI output dependency. Rejected System by behavior text: purely local recalculation semantics.
behaviors.test_description: With real engine modules wired, set `A1=3`, `B1='=A1*2'`, `C1='=B1+1'`, recalc, assert `B1=6` and `C1=7`; then change A1 and verify both recompute correctly.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Circular detection is a graph-evaluation interaction failure, not a single local helper. Unit tests of a detector function can miss real engine traversal/state bugs. No System/Agentic/Workflow triggers in behavior text. Rejected System because no external infra condition is required.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `A1='=B1+1'` and `B1='=A1+1'`, run evaluation with real dependency tracking, assert explicit circular-reference error and bounded execution (no hang/infinite recursion).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is formula parsing + table-range extraction + VLOOKUP function semantics. A standalone VLOOKUP algorithm unit test may hide range/addressing mismatches. No OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency. Rejected System due purely local formula behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Populate lookup table cells, evaluate a `VLOOKUP(..., FALSE)` exact-match formula and a missing-key formula through the full engine, assert correct returned value and correct not-found error code.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Behavior focuses on expression/function logic for nested branching in one formula; minimum real failure boundary is evaluator logic itself. No required cross-module state handoff beyond a single evaluation context. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require multi-module orchestration beyond local AST/eval correctness.
behaviors.test_description: At function/evaluator unit scope, evaluate nested IF expression against representative A1 values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`; include branch short-circuit check if supported.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is function semantics (string join + empty handling). This can be caught at local function/evaluator boundary without multi-module wiring. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about CONCATENATE output rules, not dependency propagation or module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test CONCATENATE evaluation with argument lists containing normal strings and empties, assert separator placement and omission behavior for empty inputs.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet resolution is explicit module handoff (workbook/sheet namespace resolver + evaluator). Unit on parser or arithmetic alone would miss lookup wiring failures. No System/Agentic/Workflow conditions in wording. Rejected System: no OS/network/subprocess dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Build a workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set `Sheet2!A1`, set `Sheet1!A1='=Sheet2!A1+1'`, run real recalculation, assert resolved value.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets dependency propagation/order across modules (graph maintenance, invalidation, scheduler, evaluator). Unit tests cannot expose handoff/order bugs reliably. No real external infra or AI dependency. Rejected System because ordering failure is internal engine logic, not OS/network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Create a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1 -> B1/C1 -> D1), compute once, mutate A1, trigger recalc, assert updated values and that recomputation order respects DAG dependencies (via observable hooks/log or deterministic outputs requiring correct order).

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness depends on interaction between edit history/state snapshots and recalculation graph/value cache restoration. Unit-testing only stack mechanics misses cross-module restore bugs. No System/Agentic/Workflow requirement. Rejected System because behavior does not require subprocess/OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Using real engine state/history modules, edit a formula affecting dependents, verify changed outputs, perform undo, assert original formula text restored and dependent computed values exactly restored.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is serializer/deserializer + workbook model roundtrip; this is multi-module local persistence. REAL-INFRA check: behavior needs file persistence but not OS-specific event/subprocess/network behavior, so System is not strictly required. Rejected System based on behavior text focusing on data fidelity, not OS/file-watcher semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and floating-point edge values, save via real binary serializer to a temp file, reload with real loader, assert formulas and evaluated values match original within exact/required precision guarantees.
